source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: Humanoid robots will be ubiquitous in homes and factories within 10 years
summary_extended: Baker makes a high-conviction 10-year prediction that humanoid robots will be ubiquitous in both homes and factories, representing a fundamental shift in how physical labor is performed. He cites Tesla's Optimus robot as a concrete example, extending the vision further to humanoid robots eventually mining asteroids as a longer-term frontier. The thesis is framed alongside his prediction of artificial superintelligence (ASI) as one of the most transformative structural changes of the coming decade. While Baker does not name specific investment vehicles in this segment, the implication is that companies developing humanoid robotics platforms — particularly Tesla with Optimus — stand to benefit enormously. Investors should monitor the humanoid robotics space for entry points as the technology matures from prototype to mass deployment.
detail: Baker's 10-year prediction includes humanoid robots everywhere — in houses doing useful things and in factories. He mentions Optimus robots mining asteroids as a longer-term vision. This is framed as a high-conviction 10-year prediction alongside ASI.
confidence_evidence: Stated as a 10-year prediction with conviction but without specific investment names or detailed mechanism
quote_fragment: humanoid robots will be everywhere in houses and factories

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker explicitly references Optimus robots, which is Tesla's humanoid robot program, making TSLA the clearest US-listed direct beneficiary implied by this thesis even though it was not extracted in the original tickers.
